confirmations.reason: CLAUDE.md files are exactly the repo-scoped instruction files that give the agent standing instructions and context loaded at session start — a direct match.

Bundled Orchestration Skills are pre-built prompt playbooks for orchestrating tasks/spawning agents — that is a specific orchestration shortcut, not a general mechanism to bundle and load reusable capabilities/instructions. The capability describes a packaging/loading mechanism; the feature describes opinionated orchestration content.
Agent Lifecycle Hooks explicitly trigger custom logic on agent idle, task creation, and task completion events — directly matching the capability of running custom logic on agent lifecycle events.
Output Styles are saved configurations that modify the system prompt, directly shaping the agent's output style and persona — a direct realization of output/system-prompt customization.
MCP is precisely the open standard protocol for connecting external tools, data, and services — an exact match.
Managed MCP Configuration covers deploying/configuring a set of MCP servers with scoped control, allowlists, and denylists — directly realizing install, configure, scope, and discover MCP servers.
MCP Auth Methods explicitly covers env-var, HTTP header, and OAuth 2/2.1 authentication to MCP servers — a direct match to the capability.
MCP Tool Allow-listing provides granular permission control (including wildcard rules) over which MCP tools an agent may invoke — directly realizing tool governance/allow-listing.
Auto Memory (MEMORY.md) is agent-written, persisted per git repo, and auto-loaded — durable memory kept across turns/sessions, directly matching the capability.
The Subagents feature describes specialized AI assistants running in isolated context windows to handle delegated tasks — a direct match to spawning focused sub-agents.
Agent Teams describes coordinating multiple independent Claude Code sessions as a team with a lead and shared work — directly realizing multi-agent teams and messaging.

Dynamic Workflows are JavaScript scripts Claude writes ad-hoc to orchestrate subagents at scale — they are dynamically generated, not predefined repeatable named sequences saved by the user. The capability implies user-defined, saved, reusable automations; this feature is a dynamically authored script, not a saved named workflow.
Plan Mode explicitly researches and proposes changes without executing or modifying anything — a direct match to plan-then-execute mode.

Built-in Maintenance Prompt (/loop) continues unfinished work and manages PRs but is a command that resumes/continues rather than a mechanism where the agent autonomously loops until a verifiable 'done' condition is met. It does not define or check a goal condition; it is more of a continuation helper.
Worktree Isolation runs agents in separate git worktrees so parallel agents don't overwrite each other — directly realizing parallel isolated workspaces.
Claude Code on the Web runs tasks on Anthropic-managed cloud infrastructure startable from browser/phone — directly realizing a managed cloud execution environment.
Background Agent Execution runs a subagent as a non-blocking background task — a direct match to background/async execution.
Routines are saved configurations that run automatically on a schedule on Anthropic-managed cloud infrastructure — directly realizing scheduled/triggered runs.
Sandbox Runtime wraps the Claude Code process in Seatbelt/bubblewrap isolation — directly realizing sandboxed execution to limit blast radius.
Permission System is a tiered permission framework governing whether Claude Code may use specific tools — directly realizing tool permission controls.
Egress Firewall Control restricts outbound network traffic to explicitly allowed domains — a direct match to network/egress control.
Prompt Injection Protection explicitly describes safeguards against attempts to override instructions via malicious text — a direct match.
Security Guidance Plugin makes Claude review its own code changes for vulnerabilities — directly realizing security scanning of agent actions against a threat model.
Dev Container Integration runs Claude Code inside a Docker container managed by VS Code with the project mounted — directly realizing dev-container integration.
OpenTelemetry Monitoring exports metrics, events, and traces via OpenTelemetry — directly realizing audit logging/telemetry for review and compliance.
Automated PR Code Review uses multi-agent analysis to flag logic errors and security vulnerabilities in PRs — directly realizing automated code review of diffs/PRs.
Cost Tracking tracks spend and per-session token accounting — directly realizing cost and usage tracking.

Mid-Session Workflow Resume resumes a paused workflow within the same session reusing cached results — this is intra-session continuation/caching, not resuming or forking a previous (ended) session. The capability implies cross-session persistence and branching, which this feature does not provide.
Remote Control lets you continue a local Claude Code session from a phone, tablet, or browser — directly realizing remote control of agent runs.
Mobile Push Notifications sends push alerts to mobile devices for remote session events — directly realizing push notifications on run progress/completion.
Claude Code in Slack lets users delegate coding tasks by mentioning @Claude in Slack — directly realizing driving the agent from a chat surface.

Multi-Surface Connector Support describes connectors available across desktop, VS Code, web, and Claude.ai surfaces — connectors are integration mechanisms, not the access surfaces themselves. The capability is about where the agent can be used; this feature is about connector availability, which is adjacent but not the surface capability itself.
